@graph.rdfs:label: BAIV PMF Process Ontology v2.0.0
@graph.rdfs:comment: OAA-compliant Product-Market Fit process ontology with organization bridging and registry integration
@graph.schema:creator: BAIV Ontology Architect Agent
@graph.owl:versionInfo: PMF Process Ontology v2.0.0 - OAA Registry Compliant
@graph.pmf:purpose: Enable systematic PMF achievement with multi-organization support and strategic alignment

@graph.rdfs:label: Organization Context
@graph.rdfs:comment: Polymorphic organization entity supporting multiple organizational roles in PMF process
@graph.oaa:extensionRationale: Extended to support multiple organizational roles and PMF context

platformOwner.description: Organization owning and operating the PMF platform
platformOwner.role: Platform provider and PMF framework implementer

targetCustomer.description: Prospective customer organization in PMF discovery phase
targetCustomer.role: Subject of customer discovery and problem validation research

supplyChainPartner.description: Partner organization in value delivery chain

marketAnalog.description: Similar organization in adjacent or reference market
marketAnalog.role: Market validation and pattern recognition reference

pmf:requiredAttributes.orgId: Unique organization identifier linking to org:Organization
pmf:requiredAttributes.orgType: One or more organization types from defined taxonomy

@graph.rdfs:label: Organization Bridge Entity
@graph.rdfs:comment: Bridging entity connecting OAA Organization Ontology to PMF Process
@graph.pmf:bridgeFunction: Maps organization entities from org:Organization to pmf:OrganizationContext while preserving referential integrity

sourceOrgId.description: Foreign key to OAA Organization Ontology

@graph.rdfs:label: Strategy Bridge Entity
@graph.rdfs:comment: Connects organizational strategy/VSOM to PMF value proposition and product
@graph.pmf:bridgeFunction: Links org:Strategy → vsom:VSOM → pmf:ValueProposition → pmf:Product chain

@graph.rdfs:label: Product Bridge Entity
@graph.rdfs:comment: Connects value proposition to product implementation and PMF assessment

pmfProcessId.constraint: One-to-many relationship (product can have multiple PMF cycles)

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Business Rules Framework
@graph.rdfs:comment: Comprehensive business rules governing PMF process execution and validation

OR-001.rule: Platform Owner MUST be identified before PMF process instantiation

OR-002.rule: Target Customer organizations MUST have valid orgId from org:Organization

OR-003.rule: Competitor analysis REQUIRES minimum n competitor organizations identified

SR-001.rule: Value Proposition MUST trace to organizational VSOM

SR-002.rule: Product development MUST align with strategic objectives from okr:OKR

SR-003.rule: PMF metrics MUST cascade to organizational OKR key results

PR-001.rule: Customer Discovery MUST complete before Problem Validation

PR-002.rule: Sharpest Problems prioritization MUST be completed before MVP development

PR-003.rule: 3X-5X improvement validation MUST occur in PMF Testing phase

PR-004.rule: Ellis Score >=w% AND Retention >=x% required for PMF achievement declaration

DR-001.rule: Customer interview count MUST be >=n per segment

DR-002.rule: WTP analysis MUST cite Monetizing Innovation methodology

DR-003.rule: PMF metrics MUST be measured with >=m% statistical confidence

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Dependency Framework
@graph.rdfs:comment: Defines all dependencies between PMF ontology and external ontologies

organizationOntology.constraint: orgId MUST exist in org:Organization before PMF process creation

okrOntology.constraint: PMF metrics SHOULD cascade to OKR key results

vsomOntology.constraint: Value Proposition MUST derive from VSOM strategy

oaaRegistry.constraint: PMF ontology MUST be registered in OAA Registry before use

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Constraint Framework
@graph.rdfs:comment: Defines all constraints for PMF process execution and data integrity

referentialIntegrityConstraints.RI-001: orgId in pmf:OrganizationContext MUST exist in org:Organization
referentialIntegrityConstraints.RI-002: strategyId in pmf:StrategyBridge MUST exist in org:Strategy
referentialIntegrityConstraints.RI-003: vsomId in pmf:StrategyBridge MUST exist in vsom:VSOM
referentialIntegrityConstraints.RI-004: productId MUST be unique within organization scope

temporalConstraints.TC-004: PMF Assessment timestamp MUST be <= current_timestamp
temporalConstraints.TC-005: Version timestamp MUST be monotonically increasing

valueConstraints.VC-001: ellisScore MUST be decimal between 0.00 and 1.00
valueConstraints.VC-002: retentionRate MUST be decimal between 0.00 and 1.00
valueConstraints.VC-005: confidenceLevel MUST be decimal between 0.00 and 1.00

@graph.rdfs:label: Product-Market Fit
@graph.rdfs:comment: OAA-compliant PMF definition with organization context and measurement framework
@graph.pmf:definition: Product-Market Fit occurs when a product delivers significant value to a clearly defined market segment, evidenced by sustainable customer acquisition, high retention rates, strong user engagement, and customers' willingness to pay premium pricing

pmf:measurementCriteria.ellisScore: >=w% customers very disappointed if product unavailable

pmf:organizationContext.platformOwner: Organization implementing PMF process (BAIV, AIR, W4M)

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Process
@graph.rdfs:comment: Systematic OAA-governed methodology for achieving product-market fit

platformOwner.constraint: MUST be identified before process instantiation

targetCustomers.constraint: Minimum n organizations for valid market analysis

competitors.constraint: Required for competitive analysis in Customer Discovery

@graph.rdfs:label: Customer Discovery
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase 1: Systematic identification and analysis using three-layer framework

threeLayerFramework.layer1: Business Layer - CX and Monetization Model
threeLayerFramework.layer2: Solution Layer - Product form/function solving problems
threeLayerFramework.layer3: Technology Layer - Core infrastructure and capabilities

pmf:keyQuestions.section132: How prevalent is the problem? (Market size, growth, frequency)
pmf:keyQuestions.section14: What is potential value? (WTP from Monetizing Innovation)

pmf:deliverables: Customer Journey Maps with org context

@graph.rdfs:label: Problem Validation
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase 2: Verify sharpest problems with WTP analysis and 3X-5X validation

pmf:organizationRoles.targetCustomers: Provides WTP and problem severity data

pmf:willingnessToPayAnalysis.citation: Monetizing Innovation by Madhavan Ramanujam and Georg Tacke

pmf:deliverables: Problem Validation Report with orgId context
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: >=p% of target orgs rate as critical + confirmed WTP

@graph.rdfs:label: Solution Development
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase 3: Build MVP targeting sharpest problems with 3X-5X improvement

pmf:strategicAlignment.okrLink: Development OKRs cascade from org OKRs

pmf:deliverables: User Testing Results from target orgs

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Testing
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase 4: Measure PMF with org-segmented metrics
pmf:deliverables: PMF Score Dashboard by org type

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Optimization
pmf:deliverables: A/B Test Results by org segment

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Maintenance
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase 6: Monitor and preserve PMF across org ecosystem
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: Metrics maintained >=u months adapting to evolving problems

@graph.rdfs:label: OAA Registry Entry for PMF Ontology
@graph.rdfs:comment: Metadata entry in OAA Ontology Schema Registry

changes: Added OAA compliance and registry integration
Introduced polymorphic Organization entity with multiple roles
Linked to OAA Organization Ontology via orgId
Connected to OKR framework for metrics cascade
Enhanced with OAA governance and validation rules
breakingChanges: New mandatory orgId field - existing data requires orgId assignment
Business rules enforcement - may flag previously valid processes

changes: Added WTP methodology with Monetizing Innovation citation
